vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/auto-start
vBRIEFInfo.description: Auto-synthesized minimal plan for PAN-1807: Handoff agents complete fixes but nothing submits them — PAN-1800 fix sat pushed-but-PR-less while every PR inherited red CI

plan.title: Handoff agents complete fixes but nothing submits them — PAN-1800 fix sat pushed-but-PR-less while every PR inherited red CI
plan.author: panopticon-cli/auto-start

narratives.Problem:
## Evidence (2026-06-12)

A handoff conversation agent in `workspaces/feature-pan-1800` fixed the broken Playwright spec from PAN-1800 (commit `53d4ac20f`, `test(dashboard): update resource strip spec`), pushed the branch to origin, verified the target spec green locally, and reported done in its pane.

Then: nothing. No PR was opened, no `pan done`, no review request, no operator surface. Main stayed red for ~a day, every open PR (including the PAN-1803 keystone PR #1804) inherited the failure, and the fix was only discovered by manually auditing the workspace. The operator merged it by hand (`2148beaf7`).

## Gap

Handoff conversations (`pan handoff`) intentionally bypass the work-agent pipeline, but they have **no completion contract at all**. When one finishes real work on a feature branch, the work is invisible: not in `pan review pending`, not in the dashboard needs-you queue, not a PR.

## Desired behavior

When a handoff conversation working in a feature workspace pushes commits, at least one of:
- it is instructed (in the handoff kickoff template) to finish by opening a PR or running the submit flow, or
- the system detects pushed-but-unsubmitted feature branches with a recent handoff conversation and surfaces them in the dashboard needs-you / `pan review pending` as 'completed handoff work awaiting submission'.

## Related
- PAN-1790 (handoff CLI ergonomics), PAN-1802 (handoff prompt-too-long) — same flow, different gaps.

---
Flywheel-Run-Id: RUN-23
Flywheel-Filed-By: agent
narratives.Proposal: Implement the issue directly from the tracker-provided title and body.
